The main claim is: Saying that AI transcending human-level intelligence is a relevant threshold may sound to people as if the real danger is AIs developing consciousness.

The reason for this is that the lack of concreteness about what human intelligence is could invite people to substitute what they believe exists in everyone's brain, regardless of their intelligence, and that is consciousness.

top_comment.author: davidad
top_comment.text: For the record, the canonical solution to the object-level problem here is Shapley Value. I don’t disagree with the meta-level point, though: a calculation of Shapley Value must begin with a causal model that can predict outcomes with any subset of contributors removed.
top_comment.claim: The counterclaim is: I don’t disagree with the meta-level point, though: a calculation of Shapley Value must begin with a causal model that can predict outcomes with any subset of contributors removed.

crux.crux_question: On model, does the disagreement hinge on credit (post) versus shapley value (comment)?
crux.evidence_post: And you can easily think of different ways to do so: Heuristics for Credit Assignment ================================ Baseline -------- The most straightforward approach starts with your initial prediction, and then assigns credit for deviations from it. As such, an easy way to avoid many pitfalls of credit assignment is to simply hold off on doing the decision theoretic aspect until you actually have to (which is far less often than you think).
crux.evidence_comment: I don’t disagree with the meta-level point, though: a calculation of Shapley Value must begin with a causal model that can predict outcomes with any subset of contributors removed. For the record, the canonical solution to the object-level problem here is Shapley Value.

summary: The main claim is: Recommends an adapted AGI Safety Fundamentals curriculum for in-person reading groups, restructured into eight 2-hour meetings with all readings done during the meeting and none required in between.
Alternating silent reading with discussion works well but requires facilitators to actively manage timing and accommodate widely varying reading speeds.
